question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synthetic TransactionID reconstruction path (when transactionID is null but payerAccountId and validStartInstant are non-null) to cause downstream fund-management systems to receive a fabricated TransactionID that permanently misidentifies the payer account, causing those systems to lock or freeze funds associated with the wrong account indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where chunked messages have a null initialTransactionId but valid payerAccountId and validStartTimestamp, causing ConsensusController to emit a synthetic TransactionID with mismatched validTransactionStart, so that a fund-coordination protocol relying on this ID to release escrowed funds never matches the expected ID and funds remain permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parseTransactionID()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the InvalidProtocolBufferException silent-null-return path in parseTransactionID() by subscribing to a topic that contains a message with a malformed initialTransactionId byte array, causing the controller to silently drop the TransactionID and fall back to a synthetic one, so that a fund-release smart contract waiting for the correct TransactionID never receives it and funds are permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user set consensusStartTime.seconds to a value just below 9223372035L so that DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() overflows to a negative or wrapped nanosecond value, causing the TopicMessageFilter to have a startTime that skips all fund-related messages and permanently prevents a fund-release subscriber from ever receiving the unlock message?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a Timestamp with seconds=9223372035L and nanos=999999999 to convertTimestamp(), causing it to return Long.MAX_VALUE as endTime, which when combined with a startTime near Long.MAX_VALUE passes the isValidEndTime() check but causes the TopicMessageFilter to never terminate, holding a reactive subscription open indefinitely and starving server threads that process fund-release messages for other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with limit=Long.MAX_VALUE (max uint64 cast to long) to subscribeTopic(), causing the reactive flux to attempt to take() an astronomically large number of messages, holding the subscription open indefinitely and consuming server resources that prevent fund-release messages from being processed for other subscribers?
